title: trifolium repens subsp. occidentale
intro: Trifolium repens subsp. occidentale, also known as creeping clover, is a subspecies of the common clover plant, characterized by its spreading habit and small white or pink flowers. The leaves and flowers of creeping clover have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Consuming creeping clover is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Creeping clover is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
